hero_subtitle: SFTY connects and structures multiple data sources into one secure supervisory layer to support regulators’ suspicious activity detection, case management, and evidence-ready reporting.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_title: Supervisory insight falters with disconnected data
problem_description: Regulators struggle to form a clear, unified view when suspicious activity, licensing, transaction and identity data remain siloed across systems. This fragmentation slows FIU workflows and complicates evidence aggregation needed for effective case management and reporting.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY connects suspicious activity, licensing, transaction and identity data into a unified supervisory evidence layer that supports FIU workflows, case management, and regulatory reporting in a single secure platform.
workflow_title: From market signals to supervisory cases
workflow_description: SFTY aggregates diverse market data and risk signals into structured cases supported by evidence for supervisory review and regulatory reporting.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. One supervisory layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY helps regulators connect multiple data sources into structured cases, evidence and reporting within a unified supervisory environment tailored for FIU workflows.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY provides regulators a secure, auditable platform combining data aggregation, evidence-grade logging, role-based access and procurement-safe deployment from day one.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY aggregate and connect data for FIU workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how SFTY supports suspicious activity case management
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how evidence-grade logging preserves audit-ready records
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe secure deployment options for government AML infrastructure
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for regulatory authorities?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides regulators a secure supervisory layer to connect VASP data, licensing, transactions, alerts, cases and reporting into one structured, evidence-ready operating environment.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY aggregate AML and FIU data?
faq_2_answer: SFTY aggregates diverse data sources—KYT, KYC, Travel Rule, OSINT and blockchain analytics—into normalized formats linked to entities, wallets, transactions and alerts for FIU workflows.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve supervisory evidence?
faq_3_answer: The platform preserves case history, decision logs and evidence packages to support audit-ready supervisory reviews and defensible reporting workflows.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing government systems?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY supports modular integration, role-based access, audit logging and phased deployment within controlled government IT environments and workflows.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing analytics tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY connects multiple analytics and compliance providers into one coherent operational layer without replacing existing vendor tools.
final_title: Structure supervisory data into reviewable cases
final_description: See how SFTY aggregates FIU data sources into connected cases, evidence records and reporting workflows for regulatory use.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
